differentials.name: Tuberculosis
differentials.raw: Tuberculosis (in 1%): high protein content (75 g/dL), lymphocytes > 70%, positive culture (only in 20-25%)

differentials.name: Parasites: amebiasis (→ liver abscess in 15-20%), Echinococcus
differentials.raw: Parasites: amebiasis (→ liver abscess in 15-20%), Echinococcus

differentials.name: Pancreatitis / pancreatic pseudocyst / pancreaticopleural fistula (in ⅔):
differentials.raw: Pancreatitis / pancreatic pseudocyst / pancreaticopleural fistula (in ⅔):

differentials.name: Meigs-Salmon syndrome =  primary pelvic neoplasms (ovarian fibroma, thecoma, granulosa cell tumor,
differentials.raw: Meigs-Salmon syndrome =  primary pelvic neoplasms (ovarian fibroma, thecoma, granulosa cell tumor,

context: Pathogenesis: ›  pleural metastases → ⇑ pleural permeability ›  lymphatic obstruction ← pleural vessels, mediastinal nodes, thoracic duct disruption ›  bronchial obstruction → loss of volume + resorptive surface ›  hypoproteinemia ← tumor cachexia Cause:   lung cancer (26-49%), breast cancer (8-24%), lymphoma (10-28% with chylothorax in ⅔), ovarian cancer (10%), malignant mesothelioma containing hyaluronic acid (5%) Rx:   sclerosing agents: doxycycline, bleomycin, talc (C)  VASCULAR Pulmonary emboli (in 15-30% of all embolic events): √  usually left-sided pleural effusion left-sided esophageal
